signals.title: Report links Starliner problems to overconfidence and unrealistic schedules
signals.summary:
A new report links the long-running technical problems with Boeing’s CST-100 Starliner commercial crew vehicle to a combination of overconfidence, unrealistic schedules and NASA’s lack of insight into the vehicle. 
 The post  Report links Starliner problems to overconfidence and unrealistic schedules  appeared first on  SpaceNews .

Five-month-old startup Orbital has asked the Federal Communications Commission for permission to deploy up to 100,000 data center satellites, aiming to bring 10 gigawatts of computing power from space to meet rising artificial intelligence demand. 
 The post  Orbital files plans for 100,000 orbital data centers  appeared first on  SpaceNews .

FCC to vote on satellite licensing overhaul July 22
signals.summary:
The FCC is set to vote July 22 on an order to overhaul its satellite application process, creating a “licensing assembly line” to keep up with increasingly large and complex constellation plans. 
 The post  FCC to vote on satellite licensing overhaul July 22  appeared first on  SpaceNews .

signals.title: Beck: Iridium acquisition the “logical next step” for Rocket Lab
signals.summary:
Rocket Lab’s chief executive says the company’s acquisition of Iridium was the “logical next step” in his ambitions to tap into the lucrative space services market. 
 The post  Beck: Iridium acquisition the “logical next step” for Rocket Lab  appeared first on  SpaceNews .

signals.title: Oil prices on pace for fourth straight weekly loss as Trump sees progress in U.S.-Iran talks
signals.summary: Oil prices fell more than 1% on Wednesday after talks between Washington and Tehran concluded.

signals.summary: CEO Jensen Huang has called humanoid robots a “multitrillion-dollar economic opportunity.”

signals.title: NASA awards nearly $600 million in lunar lander missions
signals.summary:
NASA selected three companies to fly four lunar lander missions worth nearly $600 million as part of its lunar base ambitions, as it weighs also launching a spare Mars rover. 
 The post  NASA awards nearly $600 million in lunar lander missions  appeared first on  SpaceNews .

signals.title: Space Force fields mobile satellite-jamming system
signals.summary:
The system known as Meadowlands is transitioning to operational use as the military places greater emphasis on electronic warfare

signals.title: Vast names Isakowitz as a senior adviser
signals.summary:
Commercial space station developer Vast named the former president and chief executive of The Aerospace Corp. as an adviser while the company awaits the next phase of a NASA program. 
 The post  Vast names Isakowitz as a senior adviser  appeared first on  SpaceNews .

signals.title: Final Atlas 5 Amazon Leo mission launches
signals.summary:
An Atlas 5 lifted off July 2 carrying a set of Amazon Leo satellites in the final launch by that vehicle to carry a satellite payload.

signals.summary: Workforce reductions meant that weapon systems reach warfighters with “undocumented shortfalls related to effectiveness, suitability, [and] survivability."

signals.title: Ukraine is launching strike-drones from everything – including Black Sea robo-boats
signals.summary: The Pentagon is studying the lesson as it shifts focus toward a possible conflict with China.

signals.title: Opening a ‘Trump account’ for your children? Here is the risk you need to reckon with first.
signals.summary: The new accounts ban bonds and international stocks, forcing parents to bet their children’s financial future entirely on U.S. equities.

signals.title: Can zinc-based batteries scale into US storage buildout?
signals.summary: Support from the U.S. Department of Energy will be critical to the industry’s near-term success as customers look to lithium alternatives, International Zinc Association officials told Utility Dive.

signals.title: Eversource launches targeted load management pilots in Massachusetts
signals.summary: The programs address specific substations with high solar penetration or summer-peak congestion issues. They could expand in the coming years, a spokesperson said.

signals.title: EPA’s tougher soot standard upheld in court
signals.summary: The D.C. Circuit rejected a challenge from states and industry groups, leaving the National Ambient Air Quality Standards for fine particulate matter, including from power plants, in place as litigation over implementation continues.

signals.title: U.S. won’t renew USMCA, opening door for negotiations with Canada and Mexico
signals.summary: President Donald Trump's "primary" issue with USMCA centers on America's trade deficits with Canada and Mexico, a senior administration official said.

signals.title: Trump administration's limits on student loan forgiveness program are blocked. What to know
signals.summary: Trump's policy that narrowed eligibility for a popular student loan forgiveness program will not go into effect, after two federal judges blocked the rule.

signals.title: Trump got $15,000 in FIFA tickets from Gianni Infantino. Next up: Presenting the World Cup trophy
signals.summary: Trump’s ties to FIFA and Gianni Infantino face new scrutiny as the World Cup enters the knockout round and European lawmakers press over a Peace Prize complaint.

signals.title: Trump Accounts for kids launch July 4: What parents need to know
signals.summary: Here's a breakdown of how Trump Accounts work, who qualifies for free money and when the funds can be withdrawn.

signals.title: Russia launches massive strike on Ukraine as Poland scrambles jets, Finland restricts airspace
signals.summary: The Russian defence ministry said that it launched a "massive strike using long-range precision air, land, and sea-based weapons and attack drones."

signals.title: Apple plans five new iPhones through 2027, eyes Chinese-made chips amid foldable push, reports say
signals.summary: Apple is planning five new iPhones by 2027 and is considering Chinese memory suppliers as it expands foldable production, reports say.

signals.title: Volkswagen braces for boardroom showdown over historic cost-cutting plan
signals.summary: Volkswagen is poised for a boardroom showdown following reports that the auto giant is weighing up shutting four factories and implementing 100,000 job cuts.

signals.title: Amazon is designing its own AI chips for Echo, Fire TV and future devices, exec tells CNBC
signals.summary: Amazon hardware chief Panos Panay says the company is designing custom chips for key devices as it experiments with AI gadgets.

signals.title: Meta says WhatsApp usernames are safeguarded against scams after India flags cybersecurity risks
signals.summary: Meta has defended its WhatsApp username feature after the Indian government raised cybersecurity concerns.

signals.title: In a first, Trump will travel aboard Qatari-donated Air Force One
signals.summary: Trump will travel to North Dakota on Wednesday for the aircraft's inaugural presidential use over a week after its commissioning flights began.

signals.title: California delays emissions reporting deadline by 3 months
signals.summary: The California Air Resources Board said it will delay the compliance deadline for California’s SB 253 — the Climate Corporate Data Accountability Act — and propose “limited changes.”

signals.title: Duke gives up offshore wind lease for ‘partial’ reimbursement under Interior deal
signals.summary: A Duke Energy subsidiary paid $155 million for the lease in 2022, saying it could support up to 1.6 GW of capacity. The utility now says it will “refocus” nearly $129 million to projects that may include new nuclear, natural gas and grid enhancements.

signals.title: From wildfire risk to resilience: How asset investment tools can mitigate the growing challenge
signals.summary: A shift in asset investment planning can help utilities quantify risk and defend wildfire mitigation plans to regulators, stakeholders and the public, writes Jeff Pauska at IFS Copperleaf.

signals.title: EchoStar’s satellite TV and wireless subsidiaries file for bankruptcy
signals.summary:
EchoStar subsidiaries tied to its satellite TV and abandoned 5G network businesses have filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy protection, advancing a prepackaged restructuring plan to repay debt early after selling spectrum to SpaceX and AT&T. 
 The post  EchoStar’s satellite TV and wireless subsidiaries file for bankruptcy  appeared first on  SpaceNews .

signals.title: Latitude plans to conduct first launch from Oman
signals.summary:
French startup Latitude intends to perform the first launch of its small launch vehicle from a spaceport in Oman in late 2027. 
 The post  Latitude plans to conduct first launch from Oman  appeared first on  SpaceNews .

signals.title: Blue Origin outlines new launch pad approach as it pushes to return New Glenn to flight
signals.summary:

signals.summary: An unverified video of Ukrainian unit taking down a Shahed drone with an American-made interceptor comes as the U.S. Army seeks to field drones at scale.

signals.title: UK defense plan to unlock fresh GCAP contract before Farnborough Airshow
signals.summary: The British government came up with essential funding on the day that cash flow for the jet was due to run out.

signals.title: Germany expands its exoatmospheric missile shield with second Arrow-3 deployment
signals.summary: Kaufbeuren will be home to the system’s radar site, while the actual interceptor launchers for that battery are planned for nearby Fliegerhorst Lechfeld.

signals.title: Europe wants to rebalance trade with Beijing, but can't quit Chinese air conditioners
signals.summary: An historic heat wave has driven unprecedented demand for Chinese-made air conditioners in Europe, underscoring the tough task Brussels faces in rebalancing trade with Beijing.

signals.title: Why Japanese airlines' $400 fuel surcharges are hitting now — and when they will end
signals.summary: Fuel surcharges at Japan Airlines and All Nippon Airways increased Wednesday, despite falling jet fuel costs in Asia.

signals.title: Alibaba-affiliate Ant Group rushes into humanoid robots with a dozen deals in 18 months
signals.summary: Ant has led a 500 million yuan ($73.59 million) funding round in humanoid robotics company Zeroth, the start-up announced Thursday.

signals.title:
Inside India newsletter: Amazon, Walmart-owned Flipkart get ready to shake up
India's delivery-in-minutes sector
signals.summary: Amazon and Walmart-owned Flipkart are scrambling to expand quick commerce operations in India, not just to chase growth but to remain relevant to consumers.

signals.title: Shares of BYD and Xiaomi surge as June delivery figures fuel optimism
signals.summary: Hong Kong-listed shares of Chinese electric vehicle makers surged, after companies posted June vehicle data that helped to buoy investor sentiment.

signals.summary: The House Judiciary Committee said the South Korean government discriminated against Coupang and other U.S. companies, in a new report.

signals.title: World Cup could boost the June jobs report by 40,000, Goldman estimates

signals.summary: Second-quarter U.S. new vehicle sales show a sharp divide, with automakers offering hybrid models largely outperforming those without  them.

signals.summary: The Defence Investment Plan falls short of the £28 billion($37 billion) wanted by defense chiefs and represents a 5% increase in annual defense spending.

signals.title: The US Army goes into the breach — without soldiers

signals.title: Japanese torpedo sends US ship to the ocean floor during Valiant Shield exercise

signals.summary: Armed with loitering munitions, SOCOM MC-130J gunships and other aircraft would be able to hunt targets deep inside enemy lines.

signals.summary: Gas turbine backlogs prompted Advanced Power Services to seek changes to its project in PJM’s fast-track Reliability Resource Initiative interconnection review, but the grid operator said granting such a waiver would be unfair to other developers.

signals.title: Utilities are not spending enough on low-income household efficiency: ACEEE
signals.summary: Energy efficiency becomes more important as prices rise because it is “one of the only direct levers” a household has to reduce its bills, said Anna Johnson, senior policy manager at the American Council for an Energy-Efficient Economy.

British thermal imaging startup SatVu announced the start of commercial services June 29 for HotSat-2, marking a return to revenue-generating operations after its debut satellite failed in low Earth orbit in 2023. 
 The post  SatVu restarts commercial operations with HotSat-2  appeared first on  SpaceNews .

signals.title: USMC awards $20 million contract for first autonomous ground vehicles
signals.summary: The Marine Corps is pursuing a first-of-its-kind $20 million production contract for fully autonomous ground vehicles.

signals.title: As one Australian Army brigade bulks up with armor, its fuel and ammo needs spike
signals.summary: The formation is expected to burn through 300,000 liters of diesel per day during high-intensity operations, compared with 40,000 liters previously.

signals.title: US Army wants up to 100 drone boats to fill watercraft gap in Pacific
signals.summary: As the Army contends with a shortage of boats in the Pacific, it's looking at autonomous watercraft to fill the gaps.

signals.title: Israel sells Spyder air defense systems to Romania for $2.3 billion
signals.summary: According to manufacturer Rafael, it's the largest contract in its history.
